content.problemStatement:
You are given a list of integers heightsheightsheights representing the heights of NNN mountain peaks. For each peak, you need to find the height of the next higher peak that comes after it in the list. If there is no higher peak after a given peak, return −1-1−1 for that peak.


Input Format

The first line contains one integer nnn, the size of the list of integers.                                                                                                               - Next line contains nnn integers heights[0],heights[1]...heights[n]heights[0],heights[1]...heights[n]heights[0],heights[1]...heights[n], representing the peaks .


Output Format

For each peak, print the next higher peak that comes after it in the list. If there is no higher peak for a peak than print −1-1−1.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of mountain peaks. The second line contains N space‑separated integers heights[0], heights[1], …, heights[N‑1].
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‑separated integers. The i‑th integer is the height of the next higher peak that appears after position i, or -1 if no such peak exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. For the first peak (1) the next higher peak is 2. For the second peak (2) there is no higher peak, so -1.
Only one test case. The first 6 has no higher peak after it. 5's next higher is the last 6. 3's next higher is also the last 6. The final 6 has none.
Strictly increasing heights, so each element's next higher is the immediate next one, and the last element gets -1.

solution.approach: We need the next greater element to the right for every array position. Scan the array from left to right while maintaining a stack of indices whose next greater element has not been found yet. For the current element h[i], pop indices from the stack while h[i] > h[stack.top]; for each popped index j, set answer[j] = h[i] because h[i] is the first greater element to the right of h[j]. After processing, push i onto the stack. Elements left in the stack have no greater element, so their answer stays -1. This runs in O(N) time because each index is pushed and popped at most once, and uses O(N) auxiliary space for the answer array and the stack.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for the answer array and the stack.
